layman_headline: Crypto market is in deep fear mode — patience is the smartest play right now.
layman_explanation: The entire crypto market is experiencing extreme fear, with Bitcoin down over 6% in a week and most altcoins falling even harder — some 15-25%. This is a classic 'blood in the streets' moment where prices are falling broadly but no clear bottom has formed yet. While some coins like NEAR show a small bounce today, the overall trend is still pointing down and it's too early to catch the knife. We're watching for a real stabilization before jumping in.
layman_action: Stay in cash and wait. The market hasn't shown convincing signs of a bottom yet. Watch for Bitcoin to hold above $58,000 and the Fear & Greed index to stop worsening before committing new money.
expert_reasoning: The market is in extreme fear (18/100) and worsening — this week's average is only 16.9. BTC is at $60,040 and falling, with 50K BTC reportedly moved at a loss signaling capitulation risk per CoinTelegraph. BTC dominance at 55.8% is crushing alts. VELVET is the only strongly bullish chart but is massively overbought (RSI 70, +217% in 7 days) making it a terrible risk/reward entry. KITE has a strongly bearish chart (-2.0 bias). WLD and SKYAI have slightly bearish charts. NEAR bounced 5.4% today but lacks any chart data for technical confirmation. Without a clear technical setup with defined support and a favorable risk/reward ratio, entering any position in this environment carries excessive downside risk. The prudent move is to wait for BTC stabilization and a Fear & Greed reversal.
risk_factors: BTC capitulation risk — 50K BTC moved at a loss could trigger cascading sell pressure below $58K
Fear & Greed at 18 and still worsening — no sign of sentiment bottom yet
High BTC dominance (55.8%) means altcoins face double headwind of BTC decline plus capital rotation away from alts
watch_list: Bitcoin price action around $58,000 — a break below could accelerate selling across all alts
Fear & Greed index — watch for first day above 25 as early sign of sentiment bottoming
NEAR Protocol: if it holds above $1.75 with rising volume over next 48 hours, it becomes the top entry candidate
market_context: Crypto is in a broad risk-off phase with extreme fear, BTC down 6.5% weekly, and gold/silver selloffs dragging crypto lower. CZ attributes the sour 2026 market to AI competition, global tensions, and the typical 4-year cycle downturn.
context_banner: Extreme fear dominates crypto markets — disciplined patience now sets up superior entries when capitulation exhausts itself.

key_signals.description: Fear & Greed at 18 (Extreme Fear) and still worsening from 15 yesterday — no sentiment floor established yet, making new entries premature.
50K BTC moved at a loss signals fresh capitulation risk — historically this precedes final washout moves before bottoms form.
Gold and silver selloff is dragging Bitcoin down, indicating correlated macro deleveraging rather than crypto-specific weakness.
CZ attributes crypto's poor 2026 performance to AI competition for capital, global tensions, and the 4-year cycle — suggesting structural headwinds remain.
No swing candidate shows a volume-confirmed breakout or bullish reversal pattern — VELVET is overbought at RSI 70 after +217% and remaining candidates lack chart confirmation.
